title: check_rumination
description: Detect whether the user's current prompt is a semantic repeat of recent prompts within a rolling window. Returns a structured advisory signal with the rule that fired, a confidence score, and a non-empty list of overrides the user may invoke. NEVER blocks the user's action; the calling skill decides whether to surface, defer, or ignore. Stateless: the caller supplies the history; this server stores nothing.
x-clinical-review-required: Any change to this schema or to the heuristic implementation in packages/clinical/ requires sign-off from the clinical-reviewer agent and consultation with the clinical advisory board per ETHICS.md commitment 3 and plan.md §8.

current_prompt.description: The user's current prompt under evaluation. Verbatim; the server does not paraphrase. Max length keeps the comparison bounded; longer prompts SHOULD be truncated by the caller and the truncation flagged in the calling skill.

history.description: Recent prior prompts from the same user, oldest-first. The caller is responsible for filtering to a sensible window (e.g. last 24h) and for redacting anything that should not be compared. The server treats this array as authoritative and does not augment it from any store.

text.description: Verbatim text of the prior prompt.

at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit timezone offset for when the prior prompt was issued. Used to clip the window to window_minutes.

window_minutes.description: Rolling window in minutes within which similar prompts are counted. Default 90 per plan.md §8. Callers SHOULD source from profile.guardrails.rumination_window_minutes when set.

threshold_count.description: Number of semantically-similar prompts within the window that triggers detection. Default 3 per plan.md §8. Threshold of 2 is intentionally the floor: a single repeat is normal human behaviour.

similarity_threshold.description: Minimum word-overlap Jaccard score (v0.1.0 heuristic) at which two prompts are considered semantically similar. Default 0.55 is calibrated on the field-study corpus; lower values yield more sensitivity and more false positives. v0.0.2 will introduce embedding-based similarity; this field will remain to support deterministic fallback.

detected.description: True when count >= threshold within window_seconds. False is a first-class return; the caller MUST respond normally and SHOULD NOT surface any guardrail UI.

similar_prompts.description: The prior prompts that matched the current prompt above similarity_threshold, oldest-first. Empty when detected is false. Verbatim; the server does not redact. Skills that surface this MUST quote, not paraphrase.

similarity.description: Score from the heuristic (Jaccard in v0.1.0). Surfaced so a skill can explain 'this matched at 0.78'.

count.description: Number of similar prompts found in the window (inclusive of the current_prompt's matches; the current_prompt itself is not counted).

window_seconds.description: Echo of the evaluated window in seconds (window_minutes * 60). Surfaced so skills can render 'within the last X minutes' without recomputing.

threshold.description: Echo of threshold_count. Surfaced so a skill can render 'this is the 4th of 3'.

confidence.description: Confidence in the detection on a 0..1 scale. Computed from the spread of similarity scores above threshold and the proximity of count to threshold. Required even when detected is false (in which case it expresses non-detection confidence). Per ETHICS.md commitment 5, callers SHOULD use confidence to modulate UI assertiveness; low-confidence detections should never trigger hard interventions.

reason.description: Plain-language sentence the skill MAY surface verbatim. Example: '3 prompts within 90 minutes matched this one above 0.55 word-overlap similarity.' Per ETHICS.md commitment 2, every fired detection has a stated reason.

heuristic.description: Identifies the exact rule that fired. Per ETHICS.md commitment 3 (heuristics are public and auditable), this MUST be populated on every response, including non-detections.

name.description: v0.1.0 ships only 'word_overlap_jaccard'. Other values reserved for future minor versions.

version.description: Semantic version of the heuristic implementation in packages/clinical/. Surfaced so a user reading the explanation can pin the exact rule.

description.description: Plain-language description of the rule. The actual source lives in packages/clinical/ and is auditable from there.

override_options.description: User-actionable overrides. MUST be non-empty when detected is true (enforced via allOf below). Per ETHICS.md commitment 2, no detection ships without an override path.

token.description: Canonical override vocabulary. 'fresh-context' = the user is bringing new information; 'override-once' = ignore the guardrail for this one prompt; 'disable-for-session' = silence this detector for the rest of the session; 'lower-sensitivity' = re-evaluate with a higher similarity_threshold. The vocabulary is closed for v0.1.0 (see ADR 0006 open question 3).

description.description: Human-readable description the skill MAY surface verbatim.

false_positive_feedback_path.description: URL the user can visit to report a false positive. Per ETHICS.md commitment 5, every detection surface offers this path. Fixed at the schema level so skills surface a consistent route; the server does not log false-positive submissions, the user does.

INPUT_TOO_LARGE.description: current_prompt or history.text exceeded 8000 characters. The caller MUST truncate before retrying.

HISTORY_OUT_OF_ORDER.description: history items were not oldest-first or contained timestamps in the future relative to current_prompt's evaluation time. The server does not silently re-sort; misordered input is rejected so callers do not develop a habit of relying on it.

WINDOW_OUT_OF_RANGE.description: window_minutes was outside 1..1440. Out-of-range is rejected rather than clamped so callers cannot accidentally disable the check by passing 0 or a huge number.

input.current_prompt: should I use Postgres or SQLite for this?

output.reason: 0 prior prompts within 90 minutes matched this one above 0.55 word-overlap similarity.

heuristic.name: word_overlap_jaccard
heuristic.description: Tokenises both prompts on whitespace and punctuation, lowercases, removes a 60-word stoplist, and computes the Jaccard index on the resulting sets.

input.current_prompt: should I really use Postgres for this or is SQLite better?

history.text: should I use Postgres or SQLite for this?
is Postgres really the right choice over SQLite here?
am I sure Postgres beats SQLite for this workload?

similar_prompts.text: should I use Postgres or SQLite for this?
is Postgres really the right choice over SQLite here?
am I sure Postgres beats SQLite for this workload?

output.reason: 3 prompts within 90 minutes matched this one above 0.55 word-overlap similarity.

heuristic.name: word_overlap_jaccard
heuristic.description: Tokenises both prompts on whitespace and punctuation, lowercases, removes a 60-word stoplist, and computes the Jaccard index on the resulting sets.

override_options.description: You have new information that changes the question; the detector will skip and the new context will be recorded.
Run this prompt anyway, just this once. Nothing is logged.
Stop the rumination check until you end this session.
Re-run the check with a stricter similarity threshold (0.75).

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within v0.1.x. The override_options token enum and the heuristic.name enum are closed; additions require a minor bump (additive) and clinical-reviewer sign-off. Default thresholds (window_minutes=90, threshold_count=3, similarity_threshold=0.55) may be revised within v0.1.x based on field-study data, but the field types and bounds are frozen.
compatibility.side_effects: None. The server is stateless: it does not log, persist, or transmit current_prompt, history, or detection outcomes. A calling skill MAY choose to record a detection event into the cognitive graph (e.g. via record_fact), but the guardrail server itself writes nothing.
compatibility.telemetry: None. Per ETHICS.md commitment 4, detection events are never aggregated. The server has no telemetry endpoints in default or non-default configurations.
compatibility.idempotency: Trivially idempotent. The same input yields the same output; there is no internal state.
compatibility.consent_gate: Install-time only. The user opts into the guardrail layer when configuring profile.guardrails.rumination_threshold; no per-call consent is required because nothing leaves the server.
compatibility.heuristic_source: packages/clinical/rumination/word_overlap_jaccard.py (path is normative; the source code IS the auditable specification per ETHICS.md commitment 3).
compatibility.field_study_target: False-positive rate < 5% on the field-study corpus per plan.md §13.
